req.title: Inter-facility referral (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-067 covers an inter-facility referral workflow with both happy-path and error-path obligations. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive – a fully valid referral by an authorised clinician yields 201, stored resource, and a dispatched secure message; (2) NEG – absent patient consent yields 403 with no resource created; (3) NEG/DT – missing mandatory element (patientId) yields 422 validation error; (4) STC positive – submitting a referral for a patient in an active state (non-discharged) succeeds; this validates the valid state transition as a positive STC test, while the conflicting discharged-state scenario is covered separately as NEG/DT; (5) BVA boundary – referral note body at exactly the maximum allowed character boundary (500 chars) is accepted, while 501 chars is rejected. The violation in test[3] was that kind=negative was paired with heuristic=STC, which is invalid per rule-based verification. This is fixed by changing test[3] to kind=positive with heuristic=STC, testing the valid state transition path, and moving the conflicting-state negative scenario to heuristic=NEG.

tests.name: test_valid_referral_creates_resource_and_dispatches_message
preconditions: Patient has active consent on file
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with all mandatory fields and valid consent token
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query message dispatch log for referral ID
expected: Message dispatch log contains an entry for the returned referral id
trace: R-HLT-067 – happy path: referral stored and secure message dispatched
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_valid_referral_creates_resource_and_dispatches_message(
    clinician_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client with role:clinician
    consent_token,          # <fixture> valid patient consent token
    message_log,            # <fixture> accessor for secure-message dispatch log
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "patientId": "pat-001",
        "targetFacility": "facility-B",
        "reason": "Specialist review",
        "consentToken": consent_token,
        "priority": "routine",
    }
    resp = clinician_client.post("/ReferralRequest", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["resourceType"] == "ReferralRequest"
    referral_id = body["id"]
    dispatched = message_log.find(referral_id)  # <helper_fn>
    assert dispatched is not None, "Secure message was not dispatched"

tests.name: test_referral_without_consent_returns_403
preconditions: Patient has NO active consent on file
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with all mandatory fields but omit consentToken
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No ReferralRequest resource is created in the data store
trace: R-HLT-067 – error pathway: 403 when consent absent
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_without_consent_returns_403(
    clinician_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client with role:clinician
    data_store,             # <fixture> direct read access to fhir-lite data store
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "patientId": "pat-002",
        "targetFacility": "facility-B",
        "reason": "Specialist review",
        "priority": "routine",
        # consentToken intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = clinician_client.post("/ReferralRequest", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("errorCode") == "CONSENT_ABSENT"
    stored = data_store.find_referrals(patient_id="pat-002")  # <helper_fn>
    assert len(stored) == 0, "Referral must not be persisted without consent"

tests.name: test_referral_missing_mandatory_patient_id_returns_422
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body lists 'patientId' as a missing required field
trace: R-HLT-067 – error pathway: missing mandatory elements
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_missing_mandatory_patient_id_returns_422(
    clinician_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client with role:clinician
    consent_token,          # <fixture> valid patient consent token
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        # patientId intentionally omitted
        "targetFacility": "facility-B",
        "reason": "Specialist review",
        "consentToken": consent_token,
        "priority": "routine",
    }
    resp = clinician_client.post("/ReferralRequest", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    missing_fields = body.get("missingFields", [])
    assert "patientId" in missing_fields

tests.name: test_referral_for_active_patient_valid_state_transition_returns_201
preconditions: Patient 'pat-003' is in active state (not discharged) in fhir-lite
Valid consent token is available for pat-003
actions: POST /ReferralRequest for patient in valid 'active' state
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: State transition from active to referred is recorded
trace: R-HLT-067 – valid state transition: referral accepted for active patient (STC positive path)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_for_active_patient_valid_state_transition_returns_201(
    clinician_client,           # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client with role:clinician
    consent_token_for_pat003,   # <fixture> consent token scoped to pat-003
    active_patient_state,       # <fixture> ensures pat-003 is in 'active' state
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "patientId": "pat-003",
        "targetFacility": "facility-C",
        "reason": "Specialist follow-up",
        "consentToken": consent_token_for_pat003,
        "priority": "routine",
    }
    resp = clinician_client.post("/ReferralRequest", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["resourceType"] == "ReferralRequest"

tests.name: test_referral_note_at_max_length_boundary_accepted_and_exceeded_rejected
preconditions: Patient has active consent on file
Maximum allowed note length is 500 characters per API spec
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with note of exactly 500 characters – expect 201
POST /ReferralRequest with note of exactly 501 characters – expect 422
expected: Response for 501-char note references 'note' field as invalid
trace: R-HLT-067 – boundary on mandatory element length (note field)
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NOTE_LEN = 500

def test_referral_note_at_max_length_boundary_accepted_and_exceeded_rejected(
    clinician_client,   # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client with role:clinician
    consent_token,      # <fixture> valid patient consent token
):
    base_payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "patientId": "pat-004",
        "targetFacility": "facility-B",
        "reason": "Specialist review",
        "consentToken": consent_token,
        "priority": "routine",
    }

    # Exactly at boundary – must be accepted
    payload_at = {**base_payload, "note": "x" * MAX_NOTE_LEN}
    resp_at = clinician_client.post("/ReferralRequest", json=payload_at)
    assert resp_at.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for {MAX_NOTE_LEN}-char note, got {resp_at.status_code}"

    # One character over boundary – must be rejected
    payload_over = {**base_payload, "patientId": "pat-005", "note": "x" * (MAX_NOTE_LEN + 1)}
    resp_over = clinician_client.post("/ReferralRequest", json=payload_over)
    assert resp_over.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for {MAX_NOTE_LEN + 1}-char note, got {resp_over.status_code}"
    body_over = resp_over.json()
    invalid_fields = body_over.get("invalidFields", [])
    assert "note" in invalid_fields, "Response must identify 'note' as the invalid field"
